description: A formal document that initiates the project, defines its objectives, scope, and stakeholders, and authorizes the project manager to use organizational resources. It outlines the high-level project goals, success criteria, and constraints. Audience: Project team, stakeholders, and management.
steps_to_create: Establish project governance and decision-making processes.

description: A document that identifies potential risks to the project, assesses their likelihood and impact, and outlines mitigation strategies. It serves as a central repository for tracking and managing project risks. Audience: Project team, risk manager, and stakeholders.
steps_to_create: Assess the likelihood and impact of each risk.
Develop mitigation strategies for high-priority risks.
Assign risk owners and track progress.
Regularly review and update the risk register.

description: A document that outlines how project information will be communicated to stakeholders, including the frequency, methods, and responsible parties. It ensures that stakeholders are informed about project progress, risks, and issues. Audience: Project team, stakeholders, and management.
steps_to_create: Identify stakeholders and their communication needs.
Obtain stakeholder feedback on the communication plan.

description: A document that outlines how stakeholders will be engaged throughout the project lifecycle, including their roles, responsibilities, and communication preferences. It ensures that stakeholders are actively involved in the project and their needs are addressed. Audience: Project team, stakeholders, and management.
steps_to_create: Assess their level of interest and influence.
Develop engagement strategies for each stakeholder group.

description: A document that outlines the process for managing changes to the project scope, schedule, or budget. It ensures that changes are properly evaluated, approved, and implemented. Audience: Project team, stakeholders, and management.
steps_to_create: Define the approval process for changes.

description: A document that outlines the overall budget for the project, including funding sources, cost categories, and financial controls. It provides a high-level overview of the project's financial resources. Audience: Project team, financial stakeholders, and management.
steps_to_create: Estimate the cost of each activity.
Establish financial controls and reporting procedures.

description: A template for structuring agreements with funding partners (publishers, strategic partners, government). It outlines the legal and financial terms of the funding arrangement. Audience: Legal Counsel, Partnerships & Funding Manager, Executive Sponsors.
steps_to_create: Define the legal terms and conditions.
Outline the financial obligations of each party.
Ensure compliance with applicable laws and regulations.
approval_authorities: Legal Counsel, Executive Sponsors, Publisher Representatives

description: A high-level timeline that outlines the major project phases, milestones, and deliverables. It provides a roadmap for the project and helps track progress. Audience: Project team, stakeholders, and management.
steps_to_create: Estimate the duration of each phase.
Create a visual representation of the project timeline.

description: A framework for monitoring and evaluating the project's progress and impact. It defines the key performance indicators (KPIs), data collection methods, and reporting procedures. Audience: Project team, stakeholders, and management.
steps_to_create: Develop a plan for analyzing and using M&E data.

description: A framework outlining the approach to incorporating new and existing technologies into the game. It defines the criteria for selecting technologies, the integration process, and the risk mitigation strategies. Audience: Technical Lead, AI and Procedural Generation Lead, Project Manager.

A framework outlining the scope and characteristics of the game's open world. It defines the size, density, and diversity of the environment, as well as the gameplay opportunities it supports. Audience: Open World Director, Lead Game Designer, Art Director.
steps_to_create: Define the scope of the open world.

description: A framework outlining the extent to which the game world is generated algorithmically versus handcrafted. It defines the balance between efficiency and artistic control, as well as the methods for ensuring consistent world quality. Audience: AI and Procedural Generation Lead, Open World Director, Art Director.
steps_to_create: Define the balance between procedural and handcrafted content.
approval_authorities: AI and Procedural Generation Lead, Executive Sponsors

description: A plan outlining the sources of financial support for the game's development. It defines the strategies for securing funding from publishers, strategic partners, and government innovation grants. Audience: Partnerships & Funding Manager, Executive Sponsors, Publisher Representatives.
approval_authorities: Partnerships & Funding Manager, Executive Sponsors, Publisher Representatives

description: A framework defining the ethical framework within the game world. It outlines the player's moral choices, the consequences of their actions, and the methods for exploring complex ethical themes. Audience: Narrative Director, Lead Game Designer, Community Liaison & Tester Coordinator.
steps_to_create: Define the ethical framework of the game world.
Establish the consequences of player actions.
Develop methods for exploring ethical themes.
Gather player feedback on the morality system.

description: A framework defining the depth and structure of the game's storyline. It outlines the level of player agency and the intricacy of the narrative threads. Audience: Narrative Director, Lead Game Designer.
steps_to_create: Define the scope of the narrative.

description: A plan defining how the game will generate revenue beyond initial sales. It outlines the types of in-game purchases, subscriptions, and downloadable content offered. Audience: Partnerships & Funding Manager, Lead Game Designer.

A plan dictating the project's tolerance for risk and innovation. It controls the degree to which the project embraces experimental features and unproven technologies. Audience: Project Manager, Risk & Compliance Officer, Technical Lead.

document_name: Current State Assessment of Open World Game Technology
description: A report assessing the current state of technology relevant to open-world game development, including AI, procedural generation, graphics rendering, and physics engines. It identifies the strengths and weaknesses of available technologies and their suitability for the project. Audience: Technical Lead, AI and Procedural Generation Lead, Open World Director.
steps_to_create: Assess the strengths and weaknesses of each technology.
Evaluate the suitability of each technology for the project.
Document the findings in a report.

description: A document outlining the ethical principles and guidelines that will govern the use of AI in the game's development and content. It addresses issues such as bias, fairness, transparency, and accountability. Audience: AI and Procedural Generation Lead, Sustainability & Ethics Advisor, Legal Counsel.
steps_to_create: Adapt the framework to the specific context of game development.
Establish a process for ethical review and oversight.
Document the framework and communicate it to the development team.
approval_authorities: Sustainability & Ethics Advisor, Legal Counsel, Executive Sponsors
